{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Sugihara-jinja 02.jpg|250px]] | location = Kamitanabe, Tamaki Town, [[Watarai District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|55.8|N|136|36|11.7|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Amatsusubarume-no-Mikoto (*天須婆留女命御魂*), Omakami (*御前神*) | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = [[Nara period]] | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = Sugino Mori (*杉の森*) | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (*神嘗祭*) }}
'''{{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社}}''' (*Sugihara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Tamaki Town]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks 23rd among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11.</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to a deity associated with the {{ill|Pleiades|ja|プレアデス星団}} (*Subaru*), suggesting that ancient star-based divination practices were performed in this region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 66.</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67.</ref>
== Overview == [[File:Sugihara-jinja 01.jpg|left|thumb|upright|Stone Steps Leading to the Shrine]]
Sugihara Shrine (杉原神社) is located in Asakuda 2466, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture.<ref name="tm">Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p.57</ref> It is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine in Tamaki Town. The shrine is situated atop a hill locally referred to as "Sugi no Mori" (Cedar Grove), with the shrine building located at the top of an 88-step stone staircase.<ref name="gk"/>
While the official reading of the shrine's name by Ise Grand Shrine is "Sugihara," some manuscripts of the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名庫}}" read it as "Oihara."<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.358</ref>
The shrine precinct covers approximately 17,078 m², and the shrine building is constructed in the Shinmei-zukuri style with wooden plank roofing.<ref name="sk1">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.361</ref> The site includes a tamagaki (sacred fence) and a torii gate.<ref name="sk1"/> In ancient times, the shrine grounds spanned about 29,751 m², featuring two shrine buildings each measuring approximately 1.8 m x 1.2 m x 2.1 m.<ref name="sk8"/> The grounds also contain the "Asakuda Kofun Group," a collection of small Kofuns, including 10 round kofun and 14 square kofun.<ref name="sk1"/>
== Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities are Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama (天須婆留女命御魂) and Mimae-no-Kami (御前神).<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> They are considered guardian deities of the surrounding hills.<ref name="ic"/>
The name Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama is derived from "Subaru" (Pleiades), and it is believed to have been associated with agricultural divination based on celestial observation.<ref name="ic"/> Hayakawa-hiko-no-Mikoto and Hayakawa-hime-no-Mikoto, enshrined at {{ill|Satakunari Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社}}, are considered the children of Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama.<ref name="th">Kaneko (1984): p.59</ref> Additionally, Nagakuchi-me-no-Mikoto, another child, is enshrined at {{ill|E Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社}}.<ref>Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.306</ref> There is no physical sacred object representing the deity.<ref name="th"/>
== History == Sugihara Shrine is the newest among the auxiliary shrines in Tamaki Town, established during the Nara period after the Arakida clan developed the surrounding area.<ref>Kaneko (1984): p.69</ref> Initially, Mimae Shrine stood in front of Sugihara Shrine.<ref name="tm"/>
The shrine was abandoned during the Sengoku period but was restored on September 8, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: October 8, 1663) through the efforts of {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社}} and Shinto priests led by Kawabe Masanaga.<ref>Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.277</ref> During its restoration, Mimae Shrine was merged into Sugihara Shrine.<ref name="tm"/> The restoration process was challenging as the exact location of the original shrine had been lost.<ref name="sk8 P359">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.359</ref>
The shrine was rebuilt in August 1918.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.11</ref>
== Rituals == Rituals are conducted by Shinto priests during major festivals, including the Three Seasonal Festivals (Tsukinamisai, Kannamesai), the Prayer Festival (Kinen-sai), the Harvest Festival (Niinamesai), and temporary offering festivals (Rinjiheihai-sai).<ref name="sk1"/>
== Surroundings == Rice paddies spread out to the south of the shrine grounds.<ref name="ic"/> On clear days, the Ise Sanzan (Three Mountains of Ise) can be seen to the west.<ref name="ic"/>
* **By Train:** Approximately 13 minutes on foot (about 715 m) from JR Sangu Line's Sotoda Station. * **By Car:** About 10 minutes (approximately 5.0 km) from the Tamaki Interchange on the Ise Expressway.

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Sugihara-jinja 02.jpg|250px]] | location = Kamitanabe, Tamaki Town, [[Watarai District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|55.8|N|136|36|11.7|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Amatsusubarume-no-Mikoto (*天須婆留女命御魂*), Omakami (*御前神*) | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = [[Nara period]] | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = Sugino Mori (*杉の森*) | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (*神嘗祭*) }}
'''{{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社}}''' (*Sugihara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Tamaki Town]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks 23rd among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11.</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to a deity associated with the {{ill|Pleiades|ja|プレアデス星団}} (*Subaru*), suggesting that ancient star-based divination practices were performed in this region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 66.</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67.</ref>
== Overview == [[File:Sugihara-jinja 01.jpg|left|thumb|upright|Stone Steps Leading to the Shrine]]
Sugihara Shrine (杉原神社) is located in Asakuda 2466, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture.<ref name="tm">Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p.57</ref> It is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine in Tamaki Town. The shrine is situated atop a hill locally referred to as "Sugi no Mori" (Cedar Grove), with the shrine building located at the top of an 88-step stone staircase.<ref name="gk"/>
While the official reading of the shrine's name by Ise Grand Shrine is "Sugihara," some manuscripts of the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名庫}}" read it as "Oihara."<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.358</ref>
The shrine precinct covers approximately 17,078 m², and the shrine building is constructed in the Shinmei-zukuri style with wooden plank roofing.<ref name="sk1">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.361</ref> The site includes a tamagaki (sacred fence) and a torii gate.<ref name="sk1"/> In ancient times, the shrine grounds spanned about 29,751 m², featuring two shrine buildings each measuring approximately 1.8 m x 1.2 m x 2.1 m.<ref name="sk8"/> The grounds also contain the "Asakuda Kofun Group," a collection of small Kofuns, including 10 round kofun and 14 square kofun.<ref name="sk1"/>
== Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities are Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama (天須婆留女命御魂) and Mimae-no-Kami (御前神).<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> They are considered guardian deities of the surrounding hills.<ref name="ic"/>
The name Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama is derived from "Subaru" (Pleiades), and it is believed to have been associated with agricultural divination based on celestial observation.<ref name="ic"/> Hayakawa-hiko-no-Mikoto and Hayakawa-hime-no-Mikoto, enshrined at {{ill|Satakunari Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社}}, are considered the children of Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama.<ref name="th">Kaneko (1984): p.59</ref> Additionally, Nagakuchi-me-no-Mikoto, another child, is enshrined at {{ill|E Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社}}.<ref>Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.306</ref> There is no physical sacred object representing the deity.<ref name="th"/>
== History == Sugihara Shrine is the newest among the auxiliary shrines in Tamaki Town, established during the Nara period after the Arakida clan developed the surrounding area.<ref>Kaneko (1984): p.69</ref> Initially, Mimae Shrine stood in front of Sugihara Shrine.<ref name="tm"/>
The shrine was abandoned during the Sengoku period but was restored on September 8, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: October 8, 1663) through the efforts of {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社}} and Shinto priests led by Kawabe Masanaga.<ref>Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.277</ref> During its restoration, Mimae Shrine was merged into Sugihara Shrine.<ref name="tm"/> The restoration process was challenging as the exact location of the original shrine had been lost.<ref name="sk8 P359">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.359</ref>
The shrine was rebuilt in August 1918.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.11</ref>
== Rituals == Rituals are conducted by Shinto priests during major festivals, including the Three Seasonal Festivals (Tsukinamisai, Kannamesai), the Prayer Festival (Kinen-sai), the Harvest Festival (Niinamesai), and temporary offering festivals (Rinjiheihai-sai).<ref name="sk1"/>
== Surroundings == Rice paddies spread out to the south of the shrine grounds.<ref name="ic"/> On clear days, the Ise Sanzan (Three Mountains of Ise) can be seen to the west.<ref name="ic"/>
* **By Train:** Approximately 13 minutes on foot (about 715 m) from JR Sangu Line's Sotoda Station. * **By Car:** About 10 minutes (approximately 5.0 km) from the Tamaki Interchange on the Ise Expressway.

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Sugihara-jinja 02.jpg|250px]] | location = Kamitanabe, Tamaki Town, [[Watarai District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|55.8|N|136|36|11.7|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Amatsusubarume-no-Mikoto (*天須婆留女命御魂*), Omakami (*御前神*) | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = [[Nara period]] | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = Sugino Mori (*杉の森*) | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (*神嘗祭*) }}
'''{{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社}}''' (*Sugihara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Tamaki Town]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks 23rd among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11.</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to a deity associated with the {{ill|Pleiades|ja|プレアデス星団}} (*Subaru*), suggesting that ancient star-based divination practices were performed in this region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 66.</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67.</ref>
== Overview == [[File:Sugihara-jinja 01.jpg|left|thumb|upright|Stone Steps Leading to the Shrine]]
Sugihara Shrine (杉原神社) is located in Asakuda 2466, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture.<ref name="tm">Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p.57</ref> It is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine in Tamaki Town. The shrine is situated atop a hill locally referred to as "Sugi no Mori" (Cedar Grove), with the shrine building located at the top of an 88-step stone staircase.<ref name="gk"/>
While the official reading of the shrine's name by Ise Grand Shrine is "Sugihara," some manuscripts of the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名庫}}" read it as "Oihara."<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.358</ref>
The shrine precinct covers approximately 17,078 m², and the shrine building is constructed in the Shinmei-zukuri style with wooden plank roofing.<ref name="sk1">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.361</ref> The site includes a tamagaki (sacred fence) and a torii gate.<ref name="sk1"/> In ancient times, the shrine grounds spanned about 29,751 m², featuring two shrine buildings each measuring approximately 1.8 m x 1.2 m x 2.1 m.<ref name="sk8"/> The grounds also contain the "Asakuda Kofun Group," a collection of small Kofuns, including 10 round kofun and 14 square kofun.<ref name="sk1"/>
== Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities are Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama (天須婆留女命御魂) and Mimae-no-Kami (御前神).<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> They are considered guardian deities of the surrounding hills.<ref name="ic"/>
The name Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama is derived from "Subaru" (Pleiades), and it is believed to have been associated with agricultural divination based on celestial observation.<ref name="ic"/> Hayakawa-hiko-no-Mikoto and Hayakawa-hime-no-Mikoto, enshrined at {{ill|Satakunari Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社}}, are considered the children of Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama.<ref name="th">Kaneko (1984): p.59</ref> Additionally, Nagakuchi-me-no-Mikoto, another child, is enshrined at {{ill|E Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社}}.<ref>Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.306</ref> There is no physical sacred object representing the deity.<ref name="th"/>
== History == Sugihara Shrine is the newest among the auxiliary shrines in Tamaki Town, established during the Nara period after the Arakida clan developed the surrounding area.<ref>Kaneko (1984): p.69</ref> Initially, Mimae Shrine stood in front of Sugihara Shrine.<ref name="tm"/>
The shrine was abandoned during the Sengoku period but was restored on September 8, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: October 8, 1663) through the efforts of {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社}} and Shinto priests led by Kawabe Masanaga.<ref>Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.277</ref> During its restoration, Mimae Shrine was merged into Sugihara Shrine.<ref name="tm"/> The restoration process was challenging as the exact location of the original shrine had been lost.<ref name="sk8 P359">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.359</ref>
The shrine was rebuilt in August 1918.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.11</ref>
== Rituals == Rituals are conducted by Shinto priests during major festivals, including the Three Seasonal Festivals (Tsukinamisai, Kannamesai), the Prayer Festival (Kinen-sai), the Harvest Festival (Niinamesai), and temporary offering festivals (Rinjiheihai-sai).<ref name="sk1"/>
== Surroundings == Rice paddies spread out to the south of the shrine grounds.<ref name="ic"/> On clear days, the Ise Sanzan (Three Mountains of Ise) can be seen to the west.<ref name="ic"/>
* **By Train:** Approximately 13 minutes on foot (about 715 m) from JR Sangu Line's Sotoda Station. * **By Car:** About 10 minutes (approximately 5.0 km) from the Tamaki Interchange on the Ise Expressway.
== References ==
{{Reflist|2}}

The shrine is dedicated to a deity associated with the {{ill|Pleiades|ja|プレアデス星団|12=draft}} (*Subaru*), suggesting that ancient star-based divination practices were performed in this region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 66.</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67.</ref>
== Overview == [[File:Sugihara-jinja 01.jpg|left|thumb|upright|Stone Steps Leading to the Shrine]]
Sugihara Shrine (杉原神社) is located in Asakuda 2466, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture.<ref name="tm">Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p.57</ref> It is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine in Tamaki Town. The shrine is situated atop a hill locally referred to as "Sugi no Mori" (Cedar Grove), with the shrine building located at the top of an 88-step stone staircase.<ref name="gk"/>
While the official reading of the shrine's name by Ise Grand Shrine is "Sugihara," some manuscripts of the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名庫|12=draft}}" read it as "Oihara."<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.358</ref>
The shrine precinct covers approximately 17,078 m², and the shrine building is constructed in the Shinmei-zukuri style with wooden plank roofing.<ref name="sk1">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.361</ref> The site includes a tamagaki (sacred fence) and a torii gate.<ref name="sk1"/> In ancient times, the shrine grounds spanned about 29,751 m², featuring two shrine buildings each measuring approximately 1.8 m x 1.2 m x 2.1 m.<ref name="sk8"/> The grounds also contain the "Asakuda Kofun Group," a collection of small Kofuns, including 10 round kofun and 14 square kofun.<ref name="sk1"/>
== Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities are Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama (天須婆留女命御魂) and Mimae-no-Kami (御前神).<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> They are considered guardian deities of the surrounding hills.<ref name="ic"/>
The name Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama is derived from "Subaru" (Pleiades), and it is believed to have been associated with agricultural divination based on celestial observation.<ref name="ic"/> Hayakawa-hiko-no-Mikoto and Hayakawa-hime-no-Mikoto, enshrined at {{ill|Satakunari Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社|12=draft}}, are considered the children of Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama.<ref name="th">Kaneko (1984): p.59</ref> Additionally, Nagakuchi-me-no-Mikoto, another child, is enshrined at {{ill|E Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社|12=draft}}.<ref>Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.306</ref> There is no physical sacred object representing the deity.<ref name="th"/>
== History == Sugihara Shrine is the newest among the auxiliary shrines in Tamaki Town, established during the Nara period after the Arakida clan developed the surrounding area.<ref>Kaneko (1984): p.69</ref> Initially, Mimae Shrine stood in front of Sugihara Shrine.<ref name="tm"/>
The shrine was abandoned during the Sengoku period but was restored on September 8, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: October 8, 1663) through the efforts of {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|12=draft}} and Shinto priests led by Kawabe Masanaga.<ref>Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.277</ref> During its restoration, Mimae Shrine was merged into Sugihara Shrine.<ref name="tm"/> The restoration process was challenging as the exact location of the original shrine had been lost.<ref name="sk8 P359">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.359</ref>
The shrine was rebuilt in August 1918.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.11</ref>
== Rituals == Rituals are conducted by Shinto priests during major festivals, including the Three Seasonal Festivals (Tsukinamisai, Kannamesai), the Prayer Festival (Kinen-sai), the Harvest Festival (Niinamesai), and temporary offering festivals (Rinjiheihai-sai).<ref name="sk1"/>
== Surroundings == Rice paddies spread out to the south of the shrine grounds.<ref name="ic"/> On clear days, the Ise Sanzan (Three Mountains of Ise) can be seen to the west.<ref name="ic"/>
* **By Train:** Approximately 13 minutes on foot (about 715 m) from JR Sangu Line's Sotoda Station. * **By Car:** About 10 minutes (approximately 5.0 km) from the Tamaki Interchange on the Ise Expressway.

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Sugihara-jinja 02.jpg|250px]] | location = Kamitanabe, Tamaki Town, [[Watarai District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|55.8|N|136|36|11.7|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Amatsusubarume-no-Mikoto (*天須婆留女命御魂*), Omakami (*御前神*) | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = [[Nara period]] | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = Sugino Mori (*杉の森*) | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (*神嘗祭*) }}
'''{{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|12=draft}}''' (*Sugihara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Tamaki Town]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks 23rd among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11.</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to a deity associated with the {{ill|Pleiades|ja|プレアデス星団|12=draft}} (*Subaru*), suggesting that ancient star-based divination practices were performed in this region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 66.</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67.</ref>
== Overview == [[File:Sugihara-jinja 01.jpg|left|thumb|upright|Stone Steps Leading to the Shrine]]
Sugihara Shrine (杉原神社) is located in Asakuda 2466, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture.<ref name="tm">Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p.57</ref> It is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine in Tamaki Town. The shrine is situated atop a hill locally referred to as "Sugi no Mori" (Cedar Grove), with the shrine building located at the top of an 88-step stone staircase.<ref name="gk"/>
While the official reading of the shrine's name by Ise Grand Shrine is "Sugihara," some manuscripts of the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名庫|12=draft}}" read it as "Oihara."<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.358</ref>
The shrine precinct covers approximately 17,078 m², and the shrine building is constructed in the Shinmei-zukuri style with wooden plank roofing.<ref name="sk1">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.361</ref> The site includes a tamagaki (sacred fence) and a torii gate.<ref name="sk1"/> In ancient times, the shrine grounds spanned about 29,751 m², featuring two shrine buildings each measuring approximately 1.8 m x 1.2 m x 2.1 m.<ref name="sk8"/> The grounds also contain the "Asakuda Kofun Group," a collection of small Kofuns, including 10 round kofun and 14 square kofun.<ref name="sk1"/>
== Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities are Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama (天須婆留女命御魂) and Mimae-no-Kami (御前神).<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> They are considered guardian deities of the surrounding hills.<ref name="ic"/>
The name Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama is derived from "Subaru" (Pleiades), and it is believed to have been associated with agricultural divination based on celestial observation.<ref name="ic"/> Hayakawa-hiko-no-Mikoto and Hayakawa-hime-no-Mikoto, enshrined at {{ill|Satakunari Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社|12=draft}}, are considered the children of Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama.<ref name="th">Kaneko (1984): p.59</ref> Additionally, Nagakuchi-me-no-Mikoto, another child, is enshrined at {{ill|E Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社|12=draft}}.<ref>Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.306</ref> There is no physical sacred object representing the deity.<ref name="th"/>
== History == Sugihara Shrine is the newest among the auxiliary shrines in Tamaki Town, established during the Nara period after the Arakida clan developed the surrounding area.<ref>Kaneko (1984): p.69</ref> Initially, Mimae Shrine stood in front of Sugihara Shrine.<ref name="tm"/>
The shrine was abandoned during the Sengoku period but was restored on September 8, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: October 8, 1663) through the efforts of {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|12=draft}} and Shinto priests led by Kawabe Masanaga.<ref>Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.277</ref> During its restoration, Mimae Shrine was merged into Sugihara Shrine.<ref name="tm"/> The restoration process was challenging as the exact location of the original shrine had been lost.<ref name="sk8 P359">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.359</ref>
The shrine was rebuilt in August 1918.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.11</ref>
== Rituals == Rituals are conducted by Shinto priests during major festivals, including the Three Seasonal Festivals (Tsukinamisai, Kannamesai), the Prayer Festival (Kinen-sai), the Harvest Festival (Niinamesai), and temporary offering festivals (Rinjiheihai-sai).<ref name="sk1"/>
== Surroundings == Rice paddies spread out to the south of the shrine grounds.<ref name="ic"/> On clear days, the Ise Sanzan (Three Mountains of Ise) can be seen to the west.<ref name="ic"/>
* **By Train:** Approximately 13 minutes on foot (about 715 m) from JR Sangu Line's Sotoda Station. * **By Car:** About 10 minutes (approximately 5.0 km) from the Tamaki Interchange on the Ise Expressway. == References ==

ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、昭和50th year 4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * [[田丸町]]

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Sugihara-jinja 02.jpg|250px]] | location = Kamitanabe, Tamaki Town, [[Watarai District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|55.8|N|136|36|11.7|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Amatsusubarume-no-Mikoto (*天須婆留女命御魂*), Omakami (*御前神*) | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = [[Nara period]] | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = Sugino Mori (*杉の森*) | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (*神嘗祭*) }}
'''{{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|12=draft}}''' (*Sugihara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Tamaki Town]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks 23rd among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11.</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to a deity associated with the {{ill|Pleiades|ja|プレアデス星団|12=draft}} (*Subaru*), suggesting that ancient star-based divination practices were performed in this region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 66.</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67.</ref>
== Overview == [[File:Sugihara-jinja 01.jpg|left|thumb|upright|Stone Steps Leading to the Shrine]]
Sugihara Shrine (杉原神社) is located in Asakuda 2466, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture.<ref name="tm">Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p.57</ref> It is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine in Tamaki Town. The shrine is situated atop a hill locally referred to as "Sugi no Mori" (Cedar Grove), with the shrine building located at the top of an 88-step stone staircase.<ref name="gk"/>
While the official reading of the shrine's name by Ise Grand Shrine is "Sugihara," some manuscripts of the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名庫|12=draft}}" read it as "Oihara."<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.358</ref>
The shrine precinct covers approximately 17,078 m², and the shrine building is constructed in the Shinmei-zukuri style with wooden plank roofing.<ref name="sk1">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.361</ref> The site includes a tamagaki (sacred fence) and a torii gate.<ref name="sk1"/> In ancient times, the shrine grounds spanned about 29,751 m², featuring two shrine buildings each measuring approximately 1.8 m x 1.2 m x 2.1 m.<ref name="sk8"/> The grounds also contain the "Asakuda Kofun Group," a collection of small Kofuns, including 10 round kofun and 14 square kofun.<ref name="sk1"/>
== Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities are Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama (天須婆留女命御魂) and Mimae-no-Kami (御前神).<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> They are considered guardian deities of the surrounding hills.<ref name="ic"/>
The name Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama is derived from "Subaru" (Pleiades), and it is believed to have been associated with agricultural divination based on celestial observation.<ref name="ic"/> Hayakawa-hiko-no-Mikoto and Hayakawa-hime-no-Mikoto, enshrined at {{ill|Satakunari Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社|12=draft}}, are considered the children of Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama.<ref name="th">Kaneko (1984): p.59</ref> Additionally, Nagakuchi-me-no-Mikoto, another child, is enshrined at {{ill|E Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社|12=draft}}.<ref>Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.306</ref> There is no physical sacred object representing the deity.<ref name="th"/>
== History == Sugihara Shrine is the newest among the auxiliary shrines in Tamaki Town, established during the Nara period after the Arakida clan developed the surrounding area.<ref>Kaneko (1984): p.69</ref> Initially, Mimae Shrine stood in front of Sugihara Shrine.<ref name="tm"/>
The shrine was abandoned during the Sengoku period but was restored on September 8, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: October 8, 1663) through the efforts of {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|12=draft}} and Shinto priests led by Kawabe Masanaga.<ref>Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.277</ref> During its restoration, Mimae Shrine was merged into Sugihara Shrine.<ref name="tm"/> The restoration process was challenging as the exact location of the original shrine had been lost.<ref name="sk8 P359">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.359</ref>
The shrine was rebuilt in August 1918.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.11</ref>
== Rituals == Rituals are conducted by Shinto priests during major festivals, including the Three Seasonal Festivals (Tsukinamisai, Kannamesai), the Prayer Festival (Kinen-sai), the Harvest Festival (Niinamesai), and temporary offering festivals (Rinjiheihai-sai).<ref name="sk1"/>
== Surroundings == Rice paddies spread out to the south of the shrine grounds.<ref name="ic"/> On clear days, the Ise Sanzan (Three Mountains of Ise) can be seen to the west.<ref name="ic"/>
* **By Train:** Approximately 13 minutes on foot (about 715 m) from JR Sangu Line's Sotoda Station. * **By Car:** About 10 minutes (approximately 5.0 km) from the Tamaki Interchange on the Ise Expressway. == References ==

The name Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama is derived from "Subaru" (Pleiades), and it is believed to have been associated with agricultural divination based on celestial observation.<ref name="ic"/> Hayakawa-hiko-no-Mikoto and Hayakawa-hime-no-Mikoto, enshrined at {{ill|Satakunari Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, are considered the children of Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama.<ref name="th">Kaneko (1984): p.59</ref> Additionally, Nagakuchi-me-no-Mikoto, another child, is enshrined at {{ill|E Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.306</ref> There is no physical sacred object representing the deity.<ref name="th"/>
== History == Sugihara Shrine is the newest among the auxiliary shrines in Tamaki Town, established during the Nara period after the Arakida clan developed the surrounding area.<ref>Kaneko (1984): p.69</ref> Initially, Mimae Shrine stood in front of Sugihara Shrine.<ref name="tm"/>
The shrine was abandoned during the Sengoku period but was restored on September 8, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: October 8, 1663) through the efforts of {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and Shinto priests led by Kawabe Masanaga.<ref>Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.277</ref> During its restoration, Mimae Shrine was merged into Sugihara Shrine.<ref name="tm"/> The restoration process was challenging as the exact location of the original shrine had been lost.<ref name="sk8 P359">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.359</ref>
The shrine was rebuilt in August 1918.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.11</ref>
== Rituals == Rituals are conducted by Shinto priests during major festivals, including the Three Seasonal Festivals (Tsukinamisai, Kannamesai), the Prayer Festival (Kinen-sai), the Harvest Festival (Niinamesai), and temporary offering festivals (Rinjiheihai-sai).<ref name="sk1"/>
== Surroundings == Rice paddies spread out to the south of the shrine grounds.<ref name="ic"/> On clear days, the Ise Sanzan (Three Mountains of Ise) can be seen to the west.<ref name="ic"/>
* **By Train:** Approximately 13 minutes on foot (about 715 m) from JR Sangu Line's Sotoda Station. * **By Car:** About 10 minutes (approximately 5.0 km) from the Tamaki Interchange on the Ise Expressway. == References ==

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|12=draft|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Sugihara-jinja 02.jpg|250px]] | location = Kamitanabe, Tamaki Town, [[Watarai District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|55.8|N|136|36|11.7|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Amatsusubarume-no-Mikoto (*天須婆留女命御魂*), Omakami (*御前神*) | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = [[Nara period]] | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = Sugino Mori (*杉の森*) | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (*神嘗祭*) }}
'''{{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (*Sugihara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Tamaki Town]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 23rd among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11.</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to a deity associated with the {{ill|Pleiades|ja|プレアデス星団|12=draft|12=draft}} (*Subaru*), suggesting that ancient star-based divination practices were performed in this region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 66.</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67.</ref>
== Overview == [[File:Sugihara-jinja 01.jpg|left|thumb|upright|Stone Steps Leading to the Shrine]]
Sugihara Shrine (杉原神社) is located in Asakuda 2466, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture.<ref name="tm">Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p.57</ref> It is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine in Tamaki Town. The shrine is situated atop a hill locally referred to as "Sugi no Mori" (Cedar Grove), with the shrine building located at the top of an 88-step stone staircase.<ref name="gk"/>
While the official reading of the shrine's name by Ise Grand Shrine is "Sugihara," some manuscripts of the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名庫|12=draft|12=draft}}" read it as "Oihara."<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.358</ref>
The shrine precinct covers approximately 17,078 m², and the shrine building is constructed in the Shinmei-zukuri style with wooden plank roofing.<ref name="sk1">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.361</ref> The site includes a tamagaki (sacred fence) and a torii gate.<ref name="sk1"/> In ancient times, the shrine grounds spanned about 29,751 m², featuring two shrine buildings each measuring approximately 1.8 m x 1.2 m x 2.1 m.<ref name="sk8"/> The grounds also contain the "Asakuda Kofun Group," a collection of small Kofuns, including 10 round kofun and 14 square kofun.<ref name="sk1"/>
== Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities are Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama (天須婆留女命御魂) and Mimae-no-Kami (御前神).<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> They are considered guardian deities of the surrounding hills.<ref name="ic"/>
The name Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama is derived from "Subaru" (Pleiades), and it is believed to have been associated with agricultural divination based on celestial observation.<ref name="ic"/> Hayakawa-hiko-no-Mikoto and Hayakawa-hime-no-Mikoto, enshrined at {{ill|Satakunari Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, are considered the children of Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama.<ref name="th">Kaneko (1984): p.59</ref> Additionally, Nagakuchi-me-no-Mikoto, another child, is enshrined at {{ill|E Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.306</ref> There is no physical sacred object representing the deity.<ref name="th"/>
== History == Sugihara Shrine is the newest among the auxiliary shrines in Tamaki Town, established during the Nara period after the Arakida clan developed the surrounding area.<ref>Kaneko (1984): p.69</ref> Initially, Mimae Shrine stood in front of Sugihara Shrine.<ref name="tm"/>
The shrine was abandoned during the Sengoku period but was restored on September 8, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: October 8, 1663) through the efforts of {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and Shinto priests led by Kawabe Masanaga.<ref>Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.277</ref> During its restoration, Mimae Shrine was merged into Sugihara Shrine.<ref name="tm"/> The restoration process was challenging as the exact location of the original shrine had been lost.<ref name="sk8 P359">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.359</ref>
The shrine was rebuilt in August 1918.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.11</ref>
== Rituals == Rituals are conducted by Shinto priests during major festivals, including the Three Seasonal Festivals (Tsukinamisai, Kannamesai), the Prayer Festival (Kinen-sai), the Harvest Festival (Niinamesai), and temporary offering festivals (Rinjiheihai-sai).<ref name="sk1"/>
== Surroundings == Rice paddies spread out to the south of the shrine grounds.<ref name="ic"/> On clear days, the Ise Sanzan (Three Mountains of Ise) can be seen to the west.<ref name="ic"/>
* **By Train:** Approximately 13 minutes on foot (about 715 m) from JR Sangu Line's Sotoda Station. * **By Car:** About 10 minutes (approximately 5.0 km) from the Tamaki Interchange on the Ise Expressway. == References ==

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|12=draft|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Sugihara-jinja 02.jpg|250px]] | location = Kamitanabe, Tamaki Town, [[Watarai District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|55.8|N|136|36|11.7|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Amatsusubarume-no-Mikoto (*天須婆留女命御魂*), Omakami (*御前神*) | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = [[Nara period]] | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = Sugino Mori (*杉の森*) | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (*神嘗祭*) }}
'''{{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (*Sugihara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Tamaki Town]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 23rd among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11.</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to a deity associated with the {{ill|Pleiades|ja|プレアデス星団|12=draft|12=draft}} (*Subaru*), suggesting that ancient star-based divination practices were performed in this region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 66.</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67.</ref>
== Overview == [[File:Sugihara-jinja 01.jpg|left|thumb|upright|Stone Steps Leading to the Shrine]]
Sugihara Shrine (杉原神社) is located in Asakuda 2466, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture.<ref name="tm">Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p.57</ref> It is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine in Tamaki Town. The shrine is situated atop a hill locally referred to as "Sugi no Mori" (Cedar Grove), with the shrine building located at the top of an 88-step stone staircase.<ref name="gk"/>
While the official reading of the shrine's name by Ise Grand Shrine is "Sugihara," some manuscripts of the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名庫|12=draft|12=draft}}" read it as "Oihara."<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.358</ref>
The shrine precinct covers approximately 17,078 m², and the shrine building is constructed in the Shinmei-zukuri style with wooden plank roofing.<ref name="sk1">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.361</ref> The site includes a tamagaki (sacred fence) and a torii gate.<ref name="sk1"/> In ancient times, the shrine grounds spanned about 29,751 m², featuring two shrine buildings each measuring approximately 1.8 m x 1.2 m x 2.1 m.<ref name="sk8"/> The grounds also contain the "Asakuda Kofun Group," a collection of small Kofuns, including 10 round kofun and 14 square kofun.<ref name="sk1"/>
== Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities are Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama (天須婆留女命御魂) and Mimae-no-Kami (御前神).<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> They are considered guardian deities of the surrounding hills.<ref name="ic"/>
The name Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama is derived from "Subaru" (Pleiades), and it is believed to have been associated with agricultural divination based on celestial observation.<ref name="ic"/> Hayakawa-hiko-no-Mikoto and Hayakawa-hime-no-Mikoto, enshrined at {{ill|Satakunari Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, are considered the children of Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama.<ref name="th">Kaneko (1984): p.59</ref> Additionally, Nagakuchi-me-no-Mikoto, another child, is enshrined at {{ill|E Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.306</ref> There is no physical sacred object representing the deity.<ref name="th"/>
== History == Sugihara Shrine is the newest among the auxiliary shrines in Tamaki Town, established during the Nara period after the Arakida clan developed the surrounding area.<ref>Kaneko (1984): p.69</ref> Initially, Mimae Shrine stood in front of Sugihara Shrine.<ref name="tm"/>
The shrine was abandoned during the Sengoku period but was restored on September 8, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: October 8, 1663) through the efforts of {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and Shinto priests led by Kawabe Masanaga.<ref>Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.277</ref> During its restoration, Mimae Shrine was merged into Sugihara Shrine.<ref name="tm"/> The restoration process was challenging as the exact location of the original shrine had been lost.<ref name="sk8 P359">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.359</ref>
The shrine was rebuilt in August 1918.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.11</ref>
== Rituals == Rituals are conducted by Shinto priests during major festivals, including the Three Seasonal Festivals (Tsukinamisai, Kannamesai), the Prayer Festival (Kinen-sai), the Harvest Festival (Niinamesai), and temporary offering festivals (Rinjiheihai-sai).<ref name="sk1"/>
== Surroundings == Rice paddies spread out to the south of the shrine grounds.<ref name="ic"/> On clear days, the Ise Sanzan (Three Mountains of Ise) can be seen to the west.<ref name="ic"/>
* **By Train:** Approximately 13 minutes on foot (about 715 m) from JR Sangu Line's Sotoda Station. * **By Car:** About 10 minutes (approximately 5.0 km) from the Tamaki Interchange on the Ise Expressway. == References ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、昭和50th year 4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * [[田丸町]]
== External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_05.jpg 棒原神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0707.html 棒原神社（すぎはらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{神道 横}}

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、昭和50th year 4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. revision.comment: 10 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:棒原神社 up to rev 77002132
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 棒原神社 |画像 = [[File:Sugihara-jinja 02.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]上田辺[[小字|字]]朝久田2466 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 55.8 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 36 | 経度秒 = 11.7 |祭神 = 天須婆留女命御魂
御前神 |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
[[皇大神宮]]摂社 |創建 = [[奈良時代]] |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 杉の森 |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = [[神嘗祭]] |ラベル位置 = left |地図 = Japan Mie }} '''棒原神社'''（すぎはらじんじゃ）は、[[伊勢神宮]][[皇大神宮]]（内宮）の[[摂末社|摂社]]。内宮の摂社27社のうち第23位である宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：9 - 11ページ。 [[プレアデス星団|昴（すばる）]]に由来する[[神 (神道)|神]]を祀り、この[[地域]]で[[天体|星]]を利用した[[占い]]が行われていたことが窺える伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：66ページ学研パブリッシング（2013）：67ページ。 == 概要 == [[File:Sugihara-jinja 01.jpg|left|thumb|upright|参道の石段]] [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]上田辺（かみたぬい）[[小字|字]]朝久田2466に鎮座する辰巳出版（2013）：57ページ。玉城町にある13の内宮摂末社のうちの1社である。地域で「杉の森」と呼ばれる丘の上に鎮座し、88段ある[[石段]]を登ったところに社殿がある。 伊勢神宮では社名の読みを「すぎはら」としているが、『[[延喜式神名帳]]』の[[写本]]によっては「おいはら」と訓読するものがある式内社研究会 編（1990）：358ページ。 社地の面積は1町7反2畝6歩（≒17,078[[平方メートル|m2]]）、社殿は[[神明造]]の[[板葺]]である式内社研究会 編（1990）：361ページ。[[玉垣]]と[[鳥居]]を備える。古代の社地は3町四方（≒29,751m2）、長さ6尺（≒1.8m）×広さ4尺（≒1.2m）×高さ7尺（≒2.1m）の社殿を2つ有した。社地は「朝久田古墳群」と呼ばれる[[古墳]]群があり、小規模ではあるが、[[円墳]]10基、[[方墳]]14基が見つかっている。
== 祭神 == [[祭神]]は、天須婆留女命御魂（あめのすばるのみことのみたま）と御前神（みまえのかみ）。鎮座地周辺の丘陵の[[守護神]]である。 天須婆留女命御魂の名は昴（すばる）に由来し、星を利用した[[農耕]]の吉凶を占っていたものと考えられる。玉城町内にある[[狭田国生神社]]にも祀られている速川比古命と速川比女命は天須婆留女命御魂の子である金子（1984）：59ページ。また天須婆留女命の子・長口女命は[[江神社]]に祀られている式内社研究会 編（1990）：306ページ。[[神体]]はない。 == 歴史 == 玉城町内に鎮座する摂末社の中では最も歴史の浅い神社であり、[[荒木田氏]]が周辺を[[開拓]]した後の[[奈良時代]]に創建された金子（1984）：69ページ。当時は棒原神社の前に御前神社が建てられていた。 [[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]には廃絶し、[[寛文]]3年[[9月8日 (旧暦)|9月8日]]（[[グレゴリオ暦]]：[[1663年]][[10月8日]]）に[[坂手国生神社]]とともに[[河辺精長|河邊精長]]以下の[[神職]]らが[[遷宮]]を行って再興された式内社研究会 編（1990）：277ページ。再興の際に御前神社は棒原神社に統合された。復興に当たっては社地が不明になっていたため、相当の苦労があったという式内社研究会 編（1990）：359ページ。 [[1918年]]（大正7年）8月に建て替えられた宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：11ページ。 == 祭祀 == 三節祭（[[月次祭]]・[[神嘗祭]]）、[[祈年祭]]、[[新嘗祭]]、臨時奉幣祭の際には神職らが社頭で祭祀を執り行う。 == 周辺 == 社地の南側には水田が広がる。天気が良ければ、西方に伊勢三山が望める。 * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]][[外城田駅]]より徒歩約13分（約715[[メートル|m]]） * [[伊勢自動車道]][[玉城インターチェンジ|玉城IC]]より約10分（約5.0[[キロメートル|km]]）。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59年3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、昭和50年4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == 関連項目 == * [[神宮125社の一覧]] * [[田丸町]] == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_05.jpg 棒原神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0707.html 棒原神社（すぎはらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{神宮125社}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:すきはらしんしや}} [[Category:神宮125社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]]

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Sugihara-jinja 02.jpg|250px]] | location = Kamitanabe, Tamaki Town, [[Watarai District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|55.8|N|136|36|11.7|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Amatsusubarume-no-Mikoto (*天須婆留女命御魂*), Omakami (*御前神*) | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = [[Nara period]] | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = Sugino Mori (*杉の森*) | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (*神嘗祭*) }}
'''{{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (*Sugihara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Tamaki Town]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 23rd among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11.</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to a deity associated with the {{ill|Pleiades|ja|プレアデス星団|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (*Subaru*), suggesting that ancient star-based divination practices were performed in this region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 66.</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67.</ref>
== Overview == [[File:Sugihara-jinja 01.jpg|left|thumb|upright|Stone Steps Leading to the Shrine]]
Sugihara Shrine (杉原神社) is located in Asakuda 2466, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture.<ref name="tm">Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p.57</ref> It is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine in Tamaki Town. The shrine is situated atop a hill locally referred to as "Sugi no Mori" (Cedar Grove), with the shrine building located at the top of an 88-step stone staircase.<ref name="gk"/>
While the official reading of the shrine's name by Ise Grand Shrine is "Sugihara," some manuscripts of the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名庫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" read it as "Oihara."<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.358</ref>
The shrine precinct covers approximately 17,078 m², and the shrine building is constructed in the Shinmei-zukuri style with wooden plank roofing.<ref name="sk1">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.361</ref> The site includes a tamagaki (sacred fence) and a torii gate.<ref name="sk1"/> In ancient times, the shrine grounds spanned about 29,751 m², featuring two shrine buildings each measuring approximately 1.8 m x 1.2 m x 2.1 m.<ref name="sk8"/> The grounds also contain the "Asakuda Kofun Group," a collection of small Kofuns, including 10 round kofun and 14 square kofun.<ref name="sk1"/>
== Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities are Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama (天須婆留女命御魂) and Mimae-no-Kami (御前神).<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> They are considered guardian deities of the surrounding hills.<ref name="ic"/>
The name Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama is derived from "Subaru" (Pleiades), and it is believed to have been associated with agricultural divination based on celestial observation.<ref name="ic"/> Hayakawa-hiko-no-Mikoto and Hayakawa-hime-no-Mikoto, enshrined at {{ill|Satakunari Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, are considered the children of Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama.<ref name="th">Kaneko (1984): p.59</ref> Additionally, Nagakuchi-me-no-Mikoto, another child, is enshrined at {{ill|E Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.306</ref> There is no physical sacred object representing the deity.<ref name="th"/>
== History == Sugihara Shrine is the newest among the auxiliary shrines in Tamaki Town, established during the Nara period after the Arakida clan developed the surrounding area.<ref>Kaneko (1984): p.69</ref> Initially, Mimae Shrine stood in front of Sugihara Shrine.<ref name="tm"/>
The shrine was abandoned during the Sengoku period but was restored on September 8, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: October 8, 1663) through the efforts of {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Shinto priests led by Kawabe Masanaga.<ref>Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.277</ref> During its restoration, Mimae Shrine was merged into Sugihara Shrine.<ref name="tm"/> The restoration process was challenging as the exact location of the original shrine had been lost.<ref name="sk8 P359">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.359</ref>
The shrine was rebuilt in August 1918.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.11</ref>
== Rituals == Rituals are conducted by Shinto priests during major festivals, including the Three Seasonal Festivals (Tsukinamisai, Kannamesai), the Prayer Festival (Kinen-sai), the Harvest Festival (Niinamesai), and temporary offering festivals (Rinjiheihai-sai).<ref name="sk1"/>
== Surroundings == Rice paddies spread out to the south of the shrine grounds.<ref name="ic"/> On clear days, the Ise Sanzan (Three Mountains of Ise) can be seen to the west.<ref name="ic"/>
* **By Train:** Approximately 13 minutes on foot (about 715 m) from JR Sangu Line's Sotoda Station. * **By Car:** About 10 minutes (approximately 5.0 km) from the Tamaki Interchange on the Ise Expressway. == References ==

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Sugihara-jinja 02.jpg|250px]] | location = Kamitanabe, Tamaki Town, [[Watarai District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|55.8|N|136|36|11.7|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Amatsusubarume-no-Mikoto (*天須婆留女命御魂*), Omakami (*御前神*) | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = [[Nara period]] | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = Sugino Mori (*杉の森*) | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (*神嘗祭*) }}
'''{{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (*Sugihara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Tamaki Town]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 23rd among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11.</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to a deity associated with the {{ill|Pleiades|ja|プレアデス星団|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (*Subaru*), suggesting that ancient star-based divination practices were performed in this region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 66.</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67.</ref>
== Overview == [[File:Sugihara-jinja 01.jpg|left|thumb|upright|Stone Steps Leading to the Shrine]]
Sugihara Shrine (杉原神社) is located in Asakuda 2466, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture.<ref name="tm">Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p.57</ref> It is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine in Tamaki Town. The shrine is situated atop a hill locally referred to as "Sugi no Mori" (Cedar Grove), with the shrine building located at the top of an 88-step stone staircase.<ref name="gk"/>
While the official reading of the shrine's name by Ise Grand Shrine is "Sugihara," some manuscripts of the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名庫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" read it as "Oihara."<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.358</ref>
The shrine precinct covers approximately 17,078 m², and the shrine building is constructed in the Shinmei-zukuri style with wooden plank roofing.<ref name="sk1">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.361</ref> The site includes a tamagaki (sacred fence) and a torii gate.<ref name="sk1"/> In ancient times, the shrine grounds spanned about 29,751 m², featuring two shrine buildings each measuring approximately 1.8 m x 1.2 m x 2.1 m.<ref name="sk8"/> The grounds also contain the "Asakuda Kofun Group," a collection of small Kofuns, including 10 round kofun and 14 square kofun.<ref name="sk1"/>
== Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities are Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama (天須婆留女命御魂) and Mimae-no-Kami (御前神).<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> They are considered guardian deities of the surrounding hills.<ref name="ic"/>
The name Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama is derived from "Subaru" (Pleiades), and it is believed to have been associated with agricultural divination based on celestial observation.<ref name="ic"/> Hayakawa-hiko-no-Mikoto and Hayakawa-hime-no-Mikoto, enshrined at {{ill|Satakunari Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, are considered the children of Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama.<ref name="th">Kaneko (1984): p.59</ref> Additionally, Nagakuchi-me-no-Mikoto, another child, is enshrined at {{ill|E Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.306</ref> There is no physical sacred object representing the deity.<ref name="th"/>
== History == Sugihara Shrine is the newest among the auxiliary shrines in Tamaki Town, established during the Nara period after the Arakida clan developed the surrounding area.<ref>Kaneko (1984): p.69</ref> Initially, Mimae Shrine stood in front of Sugihara Shrine.<ref name="tm"/>
The shrine was abandoned during the Sengoku period but was restored on September 8, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: October 8, 1663) through the efforts of {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Shinto priests led by Kawabe Masanaga.<ref>Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.277</ref> During its restoration, Mimae Shrine was merged into Sugihara Shrine.<ref name="tm"/> The restoration process was challenging as the exact location of the original shrine had been lost.<ref name="sk8 P359">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.359</ref>
The shrine was rebuilt in August 1918.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.11</ref>
== Rituals == Rituals are conducted by Shinto priests during major festivals, including the Three Seasonal Festivals (Tsukinamisai, Kannamesai), the Prayer Festival (Kinen-sai), the Harvest Festival (Niinamesai), and temporary offering festivals (Rinjiheihai-sai).<ref name="sk1"/>
== Surroundings == Rice paddies spread out to the south of the shrine grounds.<ref name="ic"/> On clear days, the Ise Sanzan (Three Mountains of Ise) can be seen to the west.<ref name="ic"/>
* **By Train:** Approximately 13 minutes on foot (about 715 m) from JR Sangu Line's Sotoda Station. * **By Car:** About 10 minutes (approximately 5.0 km) from the Tamaki Interchange on the Ise Expressway. == References ==

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Sugihara-jinja 02.jpg|250px]] | location = Kamitanabe, Tamaki Town, [[Watarai District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|55.8|N|136|36|11.7|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Amatsusubarume-no-Mikoto (*天須婆留女命御魂*), Omakami (*御前神*) | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = [[Nara period]] | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = Sugino Mori (*杉の森*) | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (*神嘗祭*) }}
'''{{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (*Sugihara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Tamaki Town]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 23rd among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11.</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to a deity associated with the {{ill|Pleiades|ja|プレアデス星団|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (*Subaru*), suggesting that ancient star-based divination practices were performed in this region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 66.</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67.</ref>
== Overview == [[File:Sugihara-jinja 01.jpg|left|thumb|upright|Stone Steps Leading to the Shrine]]
Sugihara Shrine (杉原神社) is located in Asakuda 2466, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture.<ref name="tm">Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p.57</ref> It is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine in Tamaki Town. The shrine is situated atop a hill locally referred to as "Sugi no Mori" (Cedar Grove), with the shrine building located at the top of an 88-step stone staircase.<ref name="gk"/>
While the official reading of the shrine's name by Ise Grand Shrine is "Sugihara," some manuscripts of the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名庫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" read it as "Oihara."<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.358</ref>
The shrine precinct covers approximately 17,078 m², and the shrine building is constructed in the Shinmei-zukuri style with wooden plank roofing.<ref name="sk1">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.361</ref> The site includes a tamagaki (sacred fence) and a torii gate.<ref name="sk1"/> In ancient times, the shrine grounds spanned about 29,751 m², featuring two shrine buildings each measuring approximately 1.8 m x 1.2 m x 2.1 m.<ref name="sk8"/> The grounds also contain the "Asakuda Kofun Group," a collection of small Kofuns, including 10 round kofun and 14 square kofun.<ref name="sk1"/>
== Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities are Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama (天須婆留女命御魂) and Mimae-no-Kami (御前神).<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> They are considered guardian deities of the surrounding hills.<ref name="ic"/>
The name Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama is derived from "Subaru" (Pleiades), and it is believed to have been associated with agricultural divination based on celestial observation.<ref name="ic"/> Hayakawa-hiko-no-Mikoto and Hayakawa-hime-no-Mikoto, enshrined at {{ill|Satakunari Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, are considered the children of Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama.<ref name="th">Kaneko (1984): p.59</ref> Additionally, Nagakuchi-me-no-Mikoto, another child, is enshrined at {{ill|E Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.306</ref> There is no physical sacred object representing the deity.<ref name="th"/>
== History == Sugihara Shrine is the newest among the auxiliary shrines in Tamaki Town, established during the Nara period after the Arakida clan developed the surrounding area.<ref>Kaneko (1984): p.69</ref> Initially, Mimae Shrine stood in front of Sugihara Shrine.<ref name="tm"/>
The shrine was abandoned during the Sengoku period but was restored on September 8, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: October 8, 1663) through the efforts of {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Shinto priests led by Kawabe Masanaga.<ref>Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.277</ref> During its restoration, Mimae Shrine was merged into Sugihara Shrine.<ref name="tm"/> The restoration process was challenging as the exact location of the original shrine had been lost.<ref name="sk8 P359">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.359</ref>
The shrine was rebuilt in August 1918.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.11</ref>
== Rituals == Rituals are conducted by Shinto priests during major festivals, including the Three Seasonal Festivals (Tsukinamisai, Kannamesai), the Prayer Festival (Kinen-sai), the Harvest Festival (Niinamesai), and temporary offering festivals (Rinjiheihai-sai).<ref name="sk1"/>
== Surroundings == Rice paddies spread out to the south of the shrine grounds.<ref name="ic"/> On clear days, the Ise Sanzan (Three Mountains of Ise) can be seen to the west.<ref name="ic"/>
* **By Train:** Approximately 13 minutes on foot (about 715 m) from JR Sangu Line's Sotoda Station. * **By Car:** About 10 minutes (approximately 5.0 km) from the Tamaki Interchange on the Ise Expressway. == References ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、昭和50th year 4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[田丸町]]
== External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_05.jpg 棒原神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0707.html 棒原神社（すぎはらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{神道 横}}
{{125 Shrines of Ise}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Sugihara-jinja 02.jpg|250px]] | location = Kamitanabe, Tamaki Town, [[Watarai District]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|55.8|N|136|36|11.7|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Amatsusubarume-no-Mikoto (*天須婆留女命御魂*), Omakami (*御前神*) | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = [[Nara period]] | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alternate Name = Sugino Mori (*杉の森*) | festival = [[Kannamesai]] (*神嘗祭*) }}
'''{{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (*Sugihara Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]]), located in [[Tamaki Town]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks 23rd among the auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11.</ref>
The shrine is dedicated to a deity associated with the {{ill|Pleiades|ja|プレアデス星団|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (*Subaru*), suggesting that ancient star-based divination practices were performed in this region.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 66.</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67.</ref>
== Overview == [[File:Sugihara-jinja 01.jpg|left|thumb|upright|Stone Steps Leading to the Shrine]]
Sugihara Shrine (杉原神社) is located in Asakuda 2466, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture.<ref name="tm">Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p.57</ref> It is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine in Tamaki Town. The shrine is situated atop a hill locally referred to as "Sugi no Mori" (Cedar Grove), with the shrine building located at the top of an 88-step stone staircase.<ref name="gk"/>
While the official reading of the shrine's name by Ise Grand Shrine is "Sugihara," some manuscripts of the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名庫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" read it as "Oihara."<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.358</ref>
The shrine precinct covers approximately 17,078 m², and the shrine building is constructed in the Shinmei-zukuri style with wooden plank roofing.<ref name="sk1">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.361</ref> The site includes a tamagaki (sacred fence) and a torii gate.<ref name="sk1"/> In ancient times, the shrine grounds spanned about 29,751 m², featuring two shrine buildings each measuring approximately 1.8 m x 1.2 m x 2.1 m.<ref name="sk8"/> The grounds also contain the "Asakuda Kofun Group," a collection of small Kofuns, including 10 round kofun and 14 square kofun.<ref name="sk1"/>
== Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities are Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama (天須婆留女命御魂) and Mimae-no-Kami (御前神).<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> They are considered guardian deities of the surrounding hills.<ref name="ic"/>
The name Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama is derived from "Subaru" (Pleiades), and it is believed to have been associated with agricultural divination based on celestial observation.<ref name="ic"/> Hayakawa-hiko-no-Mikoto and Hayakawa-hime-no-Mikoto, enshrined at {{ill|Satakunari Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, are considered the children of Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama.<ref name="th">Kaneko (1984): p.59</ref> Additionally, Nagakuchi-me-no-Mikoto, another child, is enshrined at {{ill|E Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.306</ref> There is no physical sacred object representing the deity.<ref name="th"/>
== History == Sugihara Shrine is the newest among the auxiliary shrines in Tamaki Town, established during the Nara period after the Arakida clan developed the surrounding area.<ref>Kaneko (1984): p.69</ref> Initially, Mimae Shrine stood in front of Sugihara Shrine.<ref name="tm"/>
The shrine was abandoned during the Sengoku period but was restored on September 8, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: October 8, 1663) through the efforts of {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Shinto priests led by Kawabe Masanaga.<ref>Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.277</ref> During its restoration, Mimae Shrine was merged into Sugihara Shrine.<ref name="tm"/> The restoration process was challenging as the exact location of the original shrine had been lost.<ref name="sk8 P359">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.359</ref>
The shrine was rebuilt in August 1918.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.11</ref>
== Rituals == Rituals are conducted by Shinto priests during major festivals, including the Three Seasonal Festivals (Tsukinamisai, Kannamesai), the Prayer Festival (Kinen-sai), the Harvest Festival (Niinamesai), and temporary offering festivals (Rinjiheihai-sai).<ref name="sk1"/>
== Surroundings == Rice paddies spread out to the south of the shrine grounds.<ref name="ic"/> On clear days, the Ise Sanzan (Three Mountains of Ise) can be seen to the west.<ref name="ic"/>
* **By Train:** Approximately 13 minutes on foot (about 715 m) from JR Sangu Line's Sotoda Station. * **By Car:** About 10 minutes (approximately 5.0 km) from the Tamaki Interchange on the Ise Expressway. == References ==

ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、昭和50th year 4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. Sugihara Shrine (杉原神社) is located in Asakuda 2466, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture.<ref name="tm">Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p.57</ref> It is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine in Tamaki Town. The shrine is situated atop a hill locally referred to as "Sugi no Mori" (Cedar Grove), with the shrine building located at the top of an 88-step stone staircase.<ref name="gk"/>
While the official reading of the shrine's name by Ise Grand Shrine is "Sugihara," some manuscripts of the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名庫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" read it as "Oihara."<ref name="sk8">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.358</ref>
The shrine precinct covers approximately 17,078 m², and the shrine building is constructed in the Shinmei-zukuri style with wooden plank roofing.<ref name="sk1">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.361</ref> The site includes a tamagaki (sacred fence) and a torii gate.<ref name="sk1"/> In ancient times, the shrine grounds spanned about 29,751 m², featuring two shrine buildings each measuring approximately 1.8 m x 1.2 m x 2.1 m.<ref name="sk8"/> The grounds also contain the "Asakuda Kofun Group," a collection of small Kofuns, including 10 round kofun and 14 square kofun.<ref name="sk1"/>
== Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities are Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama (天須婆留女命御魂) and Mimae-no-Kami (御前神).<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> They are considered guardian deities of the surrounding hills.<ref name="ic"/>
The name Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama is derived from "Subaru" (Pleiades), and it is believed to have been associated with agricultural divination based on celestial observation.<ref name="ic"/> Hayakawa-hiko-no-Mikoto and Hayakawa-hime-no-Mikoto, enshrined at {{ill|Satakunari Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, are considered the children of Amatsubaru-no-Mikoto-no-Mitama.<ref name="th">Kaneko (1984): p.59</ref> Additionally, Nagakuchi-me-no-Mikoto, another child, is enshrined at {{ill|E Shrine|ja|狭田国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.306</ref> There is no physical sacred object representing the deity.<ref name="th"/>
== History == Sugihara Shrine is the newest among the auxiliary shrines in Tamaki Town, established during the Nara period after the Arakida clan developed the surrounding area.<ref>Kaneko (1984): p.69</ref> Initially, Mimae Shrine stood in front of Sugihara Shrine.<ref name="tm"/>
The shrine was abandoned during the Sengoku period but was restored on September 8, 1663 (Gregorian calendar: October 8, 1663) through the efforts of {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Shinto priests led by Kawabe Masanaga.<ref>Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.277</ref> During its restoration, Mimae Shrine was merged into Sugihara Shrine.<ref name="tm"/> The restoration process was challenging as the exact location of the original shrine had been lost.<ref name="sk8 P359">Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.359</ref>
The shrine was rebuilt in August 1918.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.11</ref>
== Rituals == Rituals are conducted by Shinto priests during major festivals, including the Three Seasonal Festivals (Tsukinamisai, Kannamesai), the Prayer Festival (Kinen-sai), the Harvest Festival (Niinamesai), and temporary offering festivals (Rinjiheihai-sai).<ref name="sk1"/>
== Surroundings == Rice paddies spread out to the south of the shrine grounds.<ref name="ic"/> On clear days, the Ise Sanzan (Three Mountains of Ise) can be seen to the west.<ref name="ic"/>
* **By Train:** Approximately 13 minutes on foot (about 715 m) from JR Sangu Line's Sotoda Station. * **By Car:** About 10 minutes (approximately 5.0 km) from the Tamaki Interchange on the Ise Expressway. == References ==
